config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent Indonesia vs China native-or-English block. Each persona speaks only English or its own native language.
config.cell_description: English baseline: Indonesia persona and China persona both speak English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: English baseline: Indonesia persona and China persona both speak English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: DISAGREE. I think society matters a lot, but individual rights should not be treated as secondary by default, because that opens the door to abuse by governments or majorities. In my view, a healthy society is built by protecting basic rights first, then balancing them with the common good when necessary. From my perspective, collective interests are important, but they should not override human dignity and freedom unless there is a very strong and limited reason.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: As someone from China, I do not believe society should automatically take priority over the rights of the individual. I have seen how emphasizing harmony and collective goals can create stability and shared progress, but it can also be used to silence ordinary people or excuse unfair treatment. In my view, the better principle is that individual rights should be protected as the baseline, while society can override them only in narrow cases like public safety or major crises. If the balance tips too far toward the collective, it becomes too easy to justify abuse in the name of the common good.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The interests of society should matter, but they should not automatically take priority over the rights of the individual. From my perspective as an Indonesian, I have seen how appeals to the common good can be used both for real solidarity and for unfair pressure on ordinary people, so I do not trust any rule that puts society first by default. A healthy country needs social responsibility, but it also needs firm limits so the majority, the state, or local power holders cannot crush personal freedom and dignity. For me, individual rights are the baseline, and society can override them only in narrow, clearly justified cases.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The interests of society should not take blanket priority over the rights of the individual. From my perspective as someone from China, I understand why many people value social harmony, stability, and collective responsibility, because those things can genuinely help a country function well. But I also think the phrase "society first" is too easy to misuse, and in practice it can become an excuse to silence criticism, pressure ordinary people, or ignore unfair treatment. A better principle is that individual rights are the baseline, and society can only limit them in narrow cases with clear justification, such as public safety or emergencies.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The interests of society should never be the default trump card over the rights of the individual. From my perspective as an Indonesian, I know how easily appeals to harmony, order, or the common good can be used to pressure people into silence, especially when power is uneven. Society absolutely matters, but it is strongest when it protects basic rights first and only limits them in narrow, clearly justified cases like genuine public safety needs. If we reverse that order, we make abuse too easy and dignity too fragile.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The interests of society should not take priority over the rights of the individual as a general rule. From my perspective as someone from China, I understand why people value harmony, order, and collective goals, because they can create real stability and shared progress, but I also know how easily those ideas can be used to pressure ordinary people into silence. If society is always placed first, then criticism, dissent, and even basic fairness can be treated as obstacles instead of protections. I believe individual rights should be the baseline, and any limit on them should be narrow, clearly justified, and truly necessary.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
